prs.title: Fix minor typos and improve backtick consistency
prs.body:
# Fix minor typos and improve documentation consistency

## Description
**This PR addresses several minor typos and consistency issues in the `ky` documentation and source code comments to ensure a high-quality, professional appearance and better readability:**

- **Standardized Terminology:** Corrected "Ky-instances" to "Ky instances" in the `readme.md` and type definitions for better style consistency.
- **Improved Syntax Highlighting:** Added missing backticks to key code symbols like `response` and `undefined` in `readme.md` code block comments.
- **Professional JSDoc Formatting:** Added missing backticks to internal error class names (`KyError`, `HTTPError`, `NetworkError`, `TimeoutError`, and `ForceRetryError`) in `source/utils/type-guards.ts` and `source/core/Ky.ts` comments.

## Type of Change
- [x] Documentation update (non-breaking change; fixes typos and formatting)

## Checklist
- [x] My changes follow the code style of this project.
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own changes.
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings.

# Thank You!

prs.title: docs: fix JSDoc for .fetch() method
prs.body:
### Description
**The JSDoc comment for the `.fetch()` method in `hono-base.ts` had a few issues:**
- Missing article: `will be entry point` → `will be the entry point`
- The `@param` tag for `ExecutionContext` was missing the parameter name (`executionCtx`), which is required by the JSDoc spec
- The `@param` and `@returns` descriptions were inconsistent with the style used by other methods in the same file (e.g. `.route()`, `.basePath()`, `.onError()`)

This PR fixes these to match the conventions already established in the codebase.

### The author should do the following, if applicable

- [ ] Add tests
- [x] Run tests
- [x] `bun run format:fix && bun run lint:fix` to format the code
- [x] Add [TSDoc](https://tsdoc.org/)/[JSDoc](https://jsdoc.app/about-getting-started) to document the code

### THANK YOU!

prs.title: Standardize error handling in CLI connection import
prs.body:
### closes: None (No related issue)

---
### Description
**This PR standardizes error handling for the `airflow connections import` CLI operation, aligning its behavior with `airflow variables import`.**

*Note: This PR was originally opened to address transaction overhead by batching commits, but based on maintainer feedback (@potiuk), it was pivoted to prioritize robust error handling over performance.*

Previously, a single database or validation error during a bulk connection import would cause the entire command to fail. 

This PR updates `connection_command.py` by wrapping the individual `session.merge()` and `session.commit()` operations inside a `try...except` block within the `_import_helper` loop. If an individual connection fails to import, it now prints the specific error, executes a `session.rollback()`, and safely continues to the next connection in the file.

* `connection_command.py`: Added per-item try/except block with `session.rollback()` to ensure single failures do not abort the entire batch import.
* `variable_command.py`: Reverted previous optimization attempts to maintain its existing, intentional per-item commit and error handling structure.

##### Was generative AI tooling used to co-author this PR?

- [ ] Yes (please specify the tool below)

---

* Read the **[Pull Request Guidelines](https://github.com/apache/airflow/blob/main/contributing-docs/05_pull_requests.rst#pull-request-guidelines)** for more information. Note: commit author/co-author name and email in commits become permanently public when merged.
* For fundamental code changes, an Airflow Improvement Proposal ([AIP](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/AIRFLOW/Airflow+Improvement+Proposals)) is needed.
* When adding dependency, check compliance with the [ASF 3rd Party License Policy](https://www.apache.org/legal/resolved.html#category-x).
* For significant user-facing changes create newsfragment: `{pr_number}.significant.rst`, in [airflow-core/newsfragments](https://github

prs.title: fix: improve duplicate module import error message
prs.body:
## Description
**While investigating the compiler analysis phase for errors or missing lines, I located an explicit `TODO fix the message here` comment in the source code (`packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/2-analyze/visitors/shared/utils.js`).**

### Triggering Code
```svelte
<script module>
	import { foo } from './somewhere.js';
</script>

<script>
	let foo = 'conflict'; // Triggers the duplicate module import error
</script>
```

## The Bug
When a component author creates a local `let` declaration in the instance `<script>` block that shadows a variable imported in the `<script module>` block, the compiler emits a `declaration_duplicate_module_import` error. 

However, the error message text was misleading. It stated:
```diff
- Cannot declare a variable with the same name as an import inside `<script module>`
+ Cannot declare a variable with the same name as an import from `<script module>`
```

This incorrectly implies that the duplicate declaration itself is happening *inside* the module script block, rather than colliding with an import *from* that block.

## Changes Made

| Action | Target (File / Function) | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Template Fix** | `packages/svelte/messages/compile-errors/script.md` | Changed "inside" to "from" to accurately reflect the nature of the shadow collision. |
| **Regenerated Errors** | `packages/svelte/src/compiler/errors.js` | Ran `pnpm generate` to apply the updated markdown template into the dictionary list. |
| **Removed TODO** | `ensure_no_module_import_conflict` function | Removed the original `// TODO fix the message here` notation from the validator. |
| **Test Adjustments** | `illegal-variable-declaration/errors.json` | Updated the validator test suite snapshots to assert against the correct error message format. |

## Validation Results

* The error correctly throws when a user replicates this component structure.
* Vitest suite `tests/validator/test.ts` successfully asserts the new, clearer message.

---
### Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following

- [x] It's really useful if your PR references an issue where it is discussed ahead of time. In many cases, features are absent for a reason. For large changes, please create an RFC: https://github.com/sveltejs/rfcs
- [x] Prefix your PR title with `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, or `docs:`.
- [x] This message body should clearly illustrate what problems it solves.
- [x] Ideally, include a test that fails without this PR but passes with it.
- [x] If this PR changes code within `packages/svelte/src`, add a changeset (`npx changeset`).

### Tests and linting

- [x] Run the tests with `pnpm test` and lint the project with `pnpm lint`

# THANK YOU

prs.title: fix: add missing RTL languages (Sindhi, Uyghur, Punjabi Shahmukhi)
prs.body:
## Description
**Sindhi (`sd`), Uyghur (`ug`), and Punjabi Shahmukhi (`pa-PK`) all use Arabic-derived scripts that are written right-to-left, but they were missing from the `rtlLanguages` array in `drawdb/src/i18n/utils/rtl.js`**

This means users browsing in these languages see a left-to-right layout instead of the correct right-to-left one.

## Changes
Added `"sd"`, `"pa-PK"`, and `"ug"` to the `rtlLanguages` array.

```diff
-const rtlLanguages = ["ar", "he", "fa", "ps", "ur"];
+const rtlLanguages = ["ar", "he", "fa", "ps", "ur", "sd", "pa-PK", "ug"];
```

## How to verify
1. Run the app locally
2. Go to **Settings → Language**
3. Switch to **Sindhi**, **Uyghur**, or **پنجابی (پاکستان)**
4. Confirm the sidebar and editor layout now renders right-to-left

## THANK YOU

prs.title: Remove alertdialog and application from non-interactive ARIA roles in…
prs.body:
## Changes

- Removes `alertdialog` and `application` from the `aria_non_interactive_roles` array in the dev toolbar. Per WAI-ARIA, these are interactive widget roles, not document structure roles.
- Fixes a minor trailing space in the `a11y-missing-attribute` error message.

## Testing

This is a static data array update, so no new tests were added. The existing `a11y-no-interactive-element-to-noninteractive-role` and `a11y-no-noninteractive-element-to-interactive-role` rules automatically pick up the corrected classifications. Verified locally that the test suite passes.

## Docs

No documentation updates needed. This strictly fixes internal dev toolbar audit behavior to align with the WAI-ARIA spec and does not affect public APIs.

# THANK YOU

prs.title: fix(a11y): add aria-hidden to skeleton component
prs.body:
# fix(a11y): add aria-hidden to skeleton component

## Description
The `Skeleton` component is a decorative loading placeholder and should be hidden from assistive technologies. This PR adds `aria-hidden="true"` to all Skeleton variants to prevent screen readers from unnecessarily announcing these elements.

This approach is consistent with how other decorative elements in the codebase, such as breadcrumb separators and navigation menu indicators, are already handled.

## Changes
**Added the `aria-hidden` attribute to the root `div` of the Skeleton component. It is placed before the prop spread to ensure developers can still override it if an edge case requires visibility.**

```diff
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 <div
   data-slot="skeleton"
+  aria-hidden="true"
   className={cn("animate-pulse rounded-md bg-accent", className)}
   {...props}
 />
```
## Affected Files

| Variant / Context | File Path |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Primary Registry** | `registry/new-york-v4/ui/skeleton.tsx` |
| **Base Registry** | `registry/bases/base/ui/skeleton.tsx` |
| **Radix Registry** | `registry/bases/radix/ui/skeleton.tsx` |
| **Base Example** | `examples/base/ui/skeleton.tsx` |
| **Radix Example** | `examples/radix/ui/skeleton.tsx` |
| **Base RTL Example** | `examples/base/ui-rtl/skeleton.tsx` |
| **Radix RTL Example** | `examples/radix/ui-rtl/skeleton.tsx` |

## Verification
- Lint passes (pnpm lint -- 1 task successful, 0 errors)
- Change applied consistently across all 7 skeleton variants
- No functional behavior change -- purely an accessibility attribute addition
- Follows existing codebase conventions for decorative elements

# THANK YOU

# **feat(i18n): complete Filipino (tl) translations to 100% coverage**

## Summary

**The Filipino (`src/i18n/locales/tl.js`) locale file was missing 130 out of 265 translation keys, making it only ~51% complete. This PR adds all missing translations, bringing the Filipino locale to full parity with the English source file.**

## Changes

**Modified:** `src/i18n/locales/tl.js`

## Added translations for the following categories:

| Category | Keys Added |
|---|---|
| Error and validation messages | ~30 |
| UI actions and labels | ~25 |
| Versioning and version control | ~15 |
| Enum support | ~10 |
| Sharing and collaboration | ~8 |
| Custom types configuration | ~8 |
| Timeline and activity log | ~8 |
| Migration support | ~6 |
| Miscellaneous UI elements | ~20 |

## Coverage

```diff
- Filipino locale: 136 / 265 keys (~51% coverage)
+ Filipino locale: 265 / 265 keys (100% coverage)
```

## Verification
- All 265 English translation keys are present in the updated file.
- File parses correctly with no syntax errors.
- Prettier formatting passes.
- ESLint passes with zero warnings.

## Notes
-- This is a single-file, atomic change.
-- All translation strings preserve the original interpolation placeholders (e.g., {{tableName}}, {{fieldName}}).
-- One pre-existing extra key (to_do) was left untouched to avoid unrelated changes.

# THANK YOU

prs.title: Optimize test efficiency in schema.test.js
prs.body:
<!-- Thanks for submitting a pull request! Please provide enough information so that others can review your pull request. The two fields below are mandatory.

If you're making a change to documentation, do **not** modify a `.html` file directly. Instead, find the corresponding `.pug` file or test case in the `test/docs` directory. -->

**Summary**

This PR optimizes the test **"booleans cause cast error for date (gh-3935)"** in `test/schema.test.js` by removing redundant calls to `test.validateSync()`.  
Previously, the test invoked `validateSync()` twice within its assertions, which was unnecessary.  
The updated approach stores the result in `validationResult` and reuses it, improving test efficiency.

**Examples**

This is a test-only optimization and does not introduce new features or modify runtime behavior.  
No additional examples are required, and the existing test continues to pass with the updated implementation.

# Pull Request: Added Kadane’s Algorithm (Maximum Subarray Sum)

## Description
This PR adds a detailed explanation and Python implementation of **Kadane’s Algorithm** — a well-known algorithm to find the **maximum sum of a contiguous subarray** within a one-dimensional array of numbers.

The document includes:
- Problem statement  
- Example input and output  
- Step-by-step intuition  
- Python implementation  
- Time and space complexity analysis  

---

## Algorithm Added
### **Kadane’s Algorithm (Maximum Subarray Sum)**

#### **Problem:**
Find the largest sum of a contiguous subarray.

Thank You!!!

prs.title: 🧩 Pull Request: Added Top 10 LeetCode Python Problems with Optimal Solutions
prs.body:
## 📝 Description
This PR adds a comprehensive set of **Top 10 LeetCode Python Problems**, covering a mix of **easy**, **medium**, and **hard** problems frequently asked in interviews.  

Each problem includes:
- 📌 **Problem Title**
- 🧠 **Explanation / Approach**
- 💡 **Python Solution**
- ⏱️ **Time Complexity**
- 💾 **Space Complexity**
- ⚙️ **Constraints**
- 🧪 **Example Inputs & Outputs**
- ✅ **Link**

This serves as a **complete learning resource** for Python-based DSA and interview preparation.

---

## 🚀 What's Added

### ✅ 1. Two Sum  
- Explained **hash map** approach.  
- **Optimal Solution:** O(n) time, O(n) space.  
- Added example and constraints.

### ✅ 2. Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock  
- Uses **sliding window** approach.  
- **Time Complexity:** O(n)  
- **Space Complexity:** O(1)

### ✅ 3. Valid Parentheses  
- Implemented using **stack** for matching brackets.  
- Handles multiple bracket types and edge cases.

### ✅ 4. Merge Two Sorted Lists  
- Both **iterative** and **recursive** approaches added.  
- Discussed **linked list merging logic** step-by-step.

### ✅ 5. Maximum Subarray (Kadane’s Algorithm)  
- Explained **dynamic approach** with dry run.  
- **Time:** O(n), **Space:** O(1)

### ✅ 6. Product of Array Except Self  
- Used **prefix and suffix arrays**.  
- Avoided division for accuracy.  
- O(n) time and O(1) extra space.

### ✅ 7. Search in Rotated Sorted Array  
- Explained **modified binary search** logic.  
- **Time:** O(log n), **Space:** O(1)

### ✅ 8. Container With Most Water  
- **Two-pointer** technique visualized and explained.  
- **Time:** O(n), **Space:** O(1)

### ✅ 9. Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters  
- Implemented with **sliding window** and **hash set**.  
- **Time:** O(n), **Space:** O(k)

### ✅ 10. Merge Intervals  
- Sort + merge logic with clear explanation.  
- **Time:** O(n log n), **Space:** O(n)

---

## 🧩 Purpose
- Enhance DSA learning with **practical Python solutions**.  
- Provide **concise explanations** and **readable code** for revision.  
- Prepare users for **technical interviews** effectively.
---

## ✅ Checklist
- [x] Added Top 10 Problems  
- [x] Added explanations and solutions  
- [x] Added constraints and examples  
- [x] Added time & space complexities  
- [x] Added proper working Leetcode Problem Links

---
# Thank You!!!

prs.title: Add Coin Change (DP) in Python
prs.body:
# 📝 Pull Request – Added Coin Change (Dynamic Programming) in Python

##  Description
This PR adds an implementation of the **Coin Change Problem** using a **Bottom-Up Dynamic Programming** approach.

The algorithm efficiently computes the **minimum number of coins** required to make up a given amount using available denominations.  
If the target amount cannot be formed, the function returns `-1`.

---

##  Approach
- Use a **1D DP array** where `dp[i]` stores the minimum number of coins needed to make amount `i`.
- Initialize all values as `amount + 1` (representing infinity) and set `dp[0] = 0`.
- For each amount `i` from `1` to `amount`, check all coin denominations and update:
- dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[i - coin] + 1)
- Return `dp[amount]` if it’s valid; otherwise, return `-1`.

## Additional Note 
- Added links to LeetCode, along with the Problem statement, Approach description, Optimal Solution, Complexity Analysis, Sample Test Cases, and Explanation.

Thank You

# Added Greedy Algorithm Question, Algorithmic Approach, Solutions, and Complexity

## 🚀 What does this PR do?
This PR adds solutions for **two classic Greedy Algorithm problems** to the repository:

1. **Assign Cookies** – Maximizes the number of content children by assigning cookies based on greed factors.
2. **Lemonade Change** – Determines if the correct change can be provided to each customer at a lemonade stand.

Both solutions are implemented in **Python** and follow a clean, beginner-friendly style with comments and proper function structure.

---

- **Folder:** Python/Greedy Algorithm
- **Files Added:**

  - `Lemonade_Change.py`

- Each file contains:
  - Problem statement
  - Greedy algorithm intuition
  - Python implementation
  - Sample inputs and outputs
  - Complexity analysis

---

## Why

These problems are **commonly asked in coding interviews** and help learners understand the **greedy algorithm approach** in a practical way. Adding them enhances the repository’s collection of beginner-friendly DSA problems.

## Description
This PR adds a self-contained **Merge Button** UI component created for the Hacktoberfest 2025 UI Component Showcase.  
The component is fully responsive, interactive,  and alerts with a surprise message.

### Features:
- Click animation (button scales when clicked)
- Hover effect (changes background and text color)
- Alert on click 
- Centered layout and responsive design

### Folder Path
projects/buttons/Vegapunk-debug/

### Live Demo
https://vegapunk-debug-merge-button-hacktober.netlify.app/

prs.title: Improved the Header / Navbar🚀
prs.body: ## 🚀 What does this PR do? This PR enhances the **website header design** for the React Projects for Beginners website, focusing on improving **usability**, **visual appeal**, and **responsiveness**. It also **fixes minor bugs related to reloading and navigation** for a smoother user experience. --- ## ✨ Key Improvements - 💡 Redesigned the header for a **cleaner and more professional look** - 📱 Provides a Liquid Glassy look during scroll - 🧭 Improved **navigation alignment**, spacing, and hover interactions - 🔄 **Resolved bugs** causing inconsistencies during page reloads - 🎯 Added a **sticky, responsive header** that remains visible while scrolling (as shown in the 3rd image) - ♿ Enhanced **accessibility** and keyboard navigation support --- ## 🖼️ Before vs After **Before:** The header (first image) was static, less structured, and not optimized for responsiveness. **After:** The new design (second & third images) features: - Provides a Liquid Glassy look during scroll - The Hactober logo fades during the scroll - Consistent alignment and spacing - Smooth scroll and hover animations - Better mobile responsiveness - Cleaner typography and modern layout - --- ## 💻 Tech Stack - **HTML** , **CSS**and**JS** (with responsive design principles) - Optimized structure for maintainability and scalability - Ready for integration with **UI frameworks** --- ## ✅ Benefits - Improved **user experience and navigation flow** - **Professional, modern, and consistent** design - Seamless behavior during **reloads and route changes** - Enhanced **mobile and tablet compatibility** --- ## 🙋‍♂️ Request Please Merge it 🌟

prs.title: Day - 4 Container With Most Water (LeetCode 11) in Python(Brute-Force-Approach)
prs.body:
### 🚀 What does this PR do?
<!-- Describe your changes in detail -->
- Adds solution for LeetCode Daily Challenge “Container With Most Water” (LeetCode 11) on Day 04 (Oct 4, 2025) in Python, Brute-Force-Approach.
- Implements the Brute-Force approach by checking all possible pairs of lines to calculate the maximum water container area.
- Iterates over each pair (i, j) where i < j, calculates the area (j - i) * min(height[i], height[j]), and keeps track of the maximum area.

### 📝 Issue Reference:
<!-- Link to the issue that your PR addresses -->
Fixes issue: #4 

### 🔍 Checklist:
Please ensure the following before submitting your PR:
- [ ] I have placed my solution in the correct folder (`solutions/dayXX/`).
- [ ] My code follows clean coding practices and is efficient.
- [ ] I have added comments in my code to explain the approach.
- [ ] I have updated the `README.md` for the specific day with a brief explanation of my solution.
- [ ] I have tested the solution on LeetCode and it works correctly.

### 🔧 Solution Details:
- **Language Used**: Python
- **Time Complexity**: O(n²) 
- **Space Complexity**: O(1)

### 🌟 Additional Notes (if any):
<!-- Add any additional info you want reviewers to know (e.g., edge cases handled, alternative approaches considered, etc.) -->
Works correctly for small inputs and handles edge cases such as arrays of length 2 or arrays with equal heights.
Not optimal for large arrays; recommended only for learning or small test cases.
Fully tested with sample inputs and custom test cases.

prs.title: Add JavaScript Solution for LeetCode 407: Trapping Rain Water II
prs.body:
### 🚀 What does this PR do?
- Adds solution for **LeetCode Daily Challenge: Trapping Rain Water II (Day 03, Date: 2025-10-03)** in **JavaScript**.
- Implements an optimal approach using a **min-heap (priority queue)** to calculate the total water trapped in a 2D elevation map.
- Traverses the map starting from boundary cells and processes neighbors to accumulate water trapped, ensuring all edge cases are handled.

### 📝 Issue Reference:
Fixes issue: # 3

### 🔍 Checklist:
Please ensure the following before submitting your PR:

- [ ] I have updated the `README.md` for the specific day with a brief explanation of my solution.

### 🔧 Solution Details:
- **Language Used**: JavaScript
- **Time Complexity**: O(m * n * log(m * n))
- **Space Complexity**: O(m * n)

prs.title: Added  Dynamic Programming Problems in Python
prs.body:
# Summary
This PR adds three classic **Dynamic Programming (DP) problems** in Python under the `Python/DynamicProgramming/` folder.  
Each problem is implemented with an **optimal approach**, includes **example usage**, **explanation**,and contains **time & space complexity analysis**.

1. Coin Change – Minimum coins to form a target amount (1D DP)
2. 0/1 Knapsack – Maximize value for a given weight limit (2D DP)
3. Longest Common Subsequence – Find LCS of two strings (2D DP)


# Changes Made
- Added `Python/DynamicProgramming/` folder for all DP problems.
- -Added file for each problem.
- Each file is **well-commented**, **clean**, and **runnable**.
- Ensures consistency across all DP implementations.
- Provides **example input/output** for quick verification.

---

# Purpose
- Help learners explore **optimal DP solutions**.
- Prepare contributors for **interview questions** and **Hacktoberfest contributions**.
---

If you would like any **changes, improvements, or additions** to these implementations or explanations, I’m happy to make them. Feedback is always welcome! 🙌

Fixes #30

issues.title: Optimize test efficiency in schema.test.js

labels.name: can't reproduce
labels.description: Mongoose devs have been unable to reproduce this issue. Close after 14 days of inactivity.

issues.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have written a descriptive issue title
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure the performance issue has not already been reported


### Last performant version

1.2.5

### Slowed down in version

1.x.x

### Node.js version

2.0.0

### 🦥 Performance issue

After upgrading to version 2.x.x, the validation step for schemas has become significantly slower.  
A test that previously ran instantly now takes approximately 200–300ms longer.  
This slowdown occurs consistently across multiple test runs.


### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install version 2.x.x of the package.
2. Create a simple schema that includes a Date field.
3. Run the following test code:

```js
const mongoose = require('mongoose');

const TestSchema = new mongoose.Schema({
  createdAt: Date
});

const Test = mongoose.model('Test', TestSchema);

// Trigger cast error by passing a boolean for a Date field
console.time('validate');
const result = new Test({ createdAt: true }).validateSync();
console.timeEnd('validate');


### Expected Behavior

The validation should run with the same performance as in version 1.x.x.  
Calling `validateSync()` should be fast and should not introduce any noticeable delay.  
There should be no slowdown when validating a schema with a Date field, even when an invalid value (such as a boolean) is provided.

issues.title: Text Heading overlaps with header in Dark Mode
issues.body: ## Issue: Button Not Visible and Text Overlaps Header in Dark Mode ### **Description** The main text appears to go **behind the header navbar**, which affects readability and layout. Additionally, when the website is switched to **Dark Mode**, the button color does not contrast well with the background, making it difficult to see. --- ### **Expected Behavior** - Page text should stay **below** the header/navbar and remain fully visible. - The button should have a **high-contrast color** in dark mode (e.g., light or accent color). --- ### **Actual Behavior** - The main text **scrolls or renders behind** the fixed navbar. ### **Screenshots (optional)** Could you please assign me to this task? I would be happy to help.

issues.title: Sidebar partially visible when toggled
issues.body: ### Issue: Sidebar partially visible when toggled **Description:** When clicking the sidebar toggle button, the sidebar is only partially hidden or slightly visible before fully sliding in. This affects the visual experience and makes the UI look glitchy. **Expected Behavior:** - The sidebar should be completely hidden off-screen when not active. - When toggled, it should slide in smoothly without any portion visible before the animation starts. **Actual Behavior:** - A small portion of the sidebar is visible even when it is supposed to be hidden. - The sliding animation feels off or unfinished. **Steps to Reproduce:** 1. Go to the website with the sidebar implemented. 2. Click the sidebar toggle button. 3. Observe the sidebar partially visible before fully sliding in. **Possible Solution:** - Ensure the sidebar's `left` (or equivalent property) is fully negative (e.g., `-250px` for a 250px width sidebar) when hidden. - Use `transition` for smooth sliding. - Check for any padding/margin that might cause it to peek. - Consider using `overflow-x: hidden` on the body or container. **Additional Context:** This issue is mainly visual and affects user experience. Fixing it will make the sidebar toggle feel smooth and professional. --- I can help fix this issue and create a smooth sliding sidebar effect with proper hiding and overlay if needed. I’d be happy to contribute this as part of open source.

issues.body: ### what's wrong with the documentation? # Improve Contributors ✨ Section UI (Circular Avatars + Clickable Hover Effect) ## Current Behavior The **Contributors ✨** section currently shows **square-shaped contributor images** with GitHub profile links written as text below each picture. This design is outdated and less interactive. ## Proposed Improvement - Change contributor images from **square → circular** (using CSS `border-radius: 50%`). - Add a **hover effect** (e.g., scale-up, subtle shadow, or border highlight). - Make the **entire contributor image clickable**, so it directly redirects to the contributor’s GitHub profile (instead of requiring a click on the text below). - Ensure the layout remains **responsive** and visually consistent. ## Expected Outcome - Cleaner and modern design for the Contributors ✨ section. - More intuitive interaction (clickable avatars). - Improved user experience and visual appeal. --- This update will **not affect the contribution process**, only enhance the presentation of contributors. ### Add screenshots #Now #After (example of the structure) ### Record - [x] I have checked the existing [issues](https://github.com/reactplay/react-play/issues) - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/reactplay/react-play/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [x] I agree to follow this project's [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/reactplay/react-play/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) - [x] I want to work on this issue - [x] I am a Hacktoberfest contributor

issues.title: Homepage background video does not loop seamlessly (visible jump/cut)
issues.body:
The background video on the homepage loops, but there is a noticeable jump/cut when it restarts.  
This makes the loop feel unnatural and distracts users.  

✅ Expected Behavior: The background video should loop seamlessly without visible cuts.  

❌ Current Behavior: When the video ends, we can see a small jump as it resets to the start.  

📌 Possible Fix:  
- Use a seamlessly edited video that aligns start and end frames.  
- Ensure encoding uses `--loop` or similar during export.  
- Alternatively, apply a CSS fade transition to hide the loop jump.  


I would be happy to help in fixing this issue in a good manner.

Thank You!
